description: When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?

Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

description: When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?

Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

description: Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for PIM-SM?
Feature Caveat: PIM-SM - Pim Active-Active is not supported on overlay VXLAN SVIs.

description: When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

description: When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Certificates?

Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

description: For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Classifiers?

Feature Caveat: Classifiers - For Classifier policy modifications to be secure, Aruba strongly encourages modifications be done as a three-step process: Bring down the port, modify, and bring the port back up.

description: Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what limitation is mentioned for Classifiers?
Feature Caveat: Classifiers - Policies containing both MAC and IPv6 classes are not allowed.

description: No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for CMF?
Feature Caveat: CMF - No other checkpoint besides "startup-configuration" gets migrated during the upgrade process.

feature: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping
description: DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping?

Feature Caveat: DHCP Server, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Snooping - DHCP Relay and DHCP Snooping can co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping and DHCP Server cannot co-exist on the same switch. DHCP Snooping, DHCP Relay, and DHCP Server together cannot co-exist on the same switch.

description: Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for IP-SLA?

Feature Caveat: IP-SLA - Reserved ports or ports used by other applications/services within the system are not recommended to be used for other services. When two services use the same port there is chance of unexpected behaviors from these services. Best practice is to use unique port for each service across system.

feature: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces
description: IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces?
Feature Caveat: IGMP/PIM on 6-in-6, Loopback and GRE interfaces - IGMP cannot be enabled on either Loopback or GRE interfaces. IGMP and PIM is not supported on a 6-in-6 Tunnel.

description: * VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX. IPv6 multicast is not supported for VXLAN overlay. Multicast support for static VXLAN in the overlay has limited support. Contact Aruba Support for details.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Multicast and VXLAN?

Feature Caveat: Multicast and VXLAN - VXLAN must be configured prior to configuring VSX. IPv6 multicast is not supported for VXLAN overlay. Multicast support for static VXLAN in the overlay has limited support. Contact Aruba Support for details.

description: Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for PFC?
Feature Caveat: PFC - Priority-based flow control (PFC) is not supported on a split port.

description: REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for REST?
Feature Caveat: REST - REST supports the 'admin' and 'operator' roles but does not work with TACACS+ command authorization.

description: Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Traceroute?
Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Issuing the traceroute command with the ip-option loosesourceroute parameter fails inan overlay EVPN-VxLAN deployment.

description: Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what limitation is mentioned for Traceroute?

Feature Caveat: Traceroute - Traceroute v4/v6 over VXLAN fails to find intermediate next-hop IP information from a source VTEP in Virtual Active Gateway environment (the SVI is the same as theActive Gateway IP).

description: Symptom: VXLAN tunnels go down after removing interfaces with IPv6 address that are the same as the VXLAN VTEP IP addresses. Scenario: In an EVPN-VXLAN deployment with an IPv6 tunnel, if any interface (irrespective of the VRF) that has same IP address as the tunnel source IP, it goes down, and then the tunnel interface is brought down. Workaround: Unconfigure loopback and VXLAN and re-configure them.

scenario: In an EVPN-VXLAN deployment with an IPv6 tunnel, if any interface (irrespective of the VRF) that has same IP address as the tunnel source IP, it goes down, and then the tunnel interface is brought down
workaround: Unconfigure loopback and VXLAN and re-configure them.

description: Symptom: If both IPv4 and IPv6 neighbors are used while configuring BGP. SNMP walk displays IPv4 in addition to other entries. description: Symptom: When an X509 certificate profile configuration with an EST profile association is pushed to a switch, it can trigger EST enrollment two times, causing the EST server to issue two certificates to the switch. Scenario: This issue has no functional impact, because only the latest enrolled certificate will take effect.

messages.content: For 9300 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what is the workaround for PKI Bug 281380?
PKI (Bug ID 281380): No workaround is documented in the release notes.

description: Symptom: A firmware upgrade from Central will fail. Scenario: This issue occurs when switch's connection to the internet is configured using the command ip source-interface http or ip source-interface all. Workaround: Configure the switch to connect to the internet without using an ip source interface.
symptom: A firmware upgrade from Central will fail.

description: Symptom: After uploading an invalid PEM certificate, the hpe-restd process becomes unstable, the WebUI temporarily stops responding, and all REST API calls from the WebUI fail. Scenario: If a user uploads a corrupt PEM certificate file using WebUI certificate management window, selecting the Upload button in the WebUI causes the WebUI to stop working completely. To recover from this state, restart hpe-restd from the bash prompt in the command-line interface or restart the switch. Workaround: Use the CLI to upload certificates.

description: Symptom: Clients are onboarding with an incorrect auth-priority order. Users onboarded with a lower-priority authentication method, so higher-priority authentication requests were not seen. Scenario: When concurrent onboarding is enabled with a default authentication priority and non-default auth-precedence on a port, clients onboarding are taking the wrong auth-priority. Workaround: At the issue state, reconfigure the auth-priority on the port to onboard the clients.
symptom: Clients are onboarding with an incorrect auth-priority order. Users onboarded with a lower-priority authentication method, so higher-priority authentication requests were not seen.

description: Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

description: Symptom: Some hpe-restd core dumps may be generated after the switch reboots. This will not interrupt the normal switch operation. Scenario: This can occur every time the switch reboots. Workaround: The hpe-restd core dumps generated after the switch is rebooted have no impact to switch operation, and can be ignored..
